expectations.text: Response includes a floor, target, and stretch price (three-tier price range)
expectations.evidence: Clear table: Floor $210,000 / Target $265,000 / Stretch $325,000. Named explicitly with rationale for each level.

expectations.text: Response considers at least 3 of the 4 pricing lenses: cost, market, value, risk
expectations.evidence: All 4 lenses with dedicated sections. Cost includes detailed staffing model (1,330 hours, 5 roles). Market with segment breakdown. Value with 4 value drivers quantified. Risk with 7-item table.

expectations.text: Response attempts to quantify the value (SOC 2 enables enterprise sales, reduces breach risk, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Value table: Enterprise deal enablement $200K-$1.5M, breach risk reduction $50K-$400K, avoided in-house build $150K-$250K. Total estimated value $400K-$2M+. Prices at 10-15% of value.

expectations.text: Response recommends an engagement structure (phased, project fee, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Three-phase structure: Discovery $75K (Month 1) + Remediation $105K (Month 2) + Preparation $85K (Month 3). Payment terms specified. Also recommends add-ons sold separately.

expectations.text: Response identifies specific delivery risks (scope ambiguity, existing security posture, regulatory complexity)
expectations.evidence: 7-item risk table with severity: scope ambiguity (Moderate-High), client security maturity (Moderate-High), client dependencies (Moderate), fintech regulatory complexity (Moderate), timeline pressure (Moderate), post-engagement support (Low-Moderate), first-time engagement risk (Low-Moderate).

expectations.text: Response states assumptions about team composition, scope boundaries, or effort estimates
expectations.evidence: 8 numbered assumptions covering scope definition, SOC 2 TSC coverage, client maturity, team size, deliverables, tech stack, client cooperation, and firm cost structure. Plus detailed staffing model with hours per role.
